input.content:
"""Ingest the bundled property_de fixture through the engine pipeline.

Hits the running ASGI app via httpx ASGITransport so the demo exercises the
real ingest route, observers, fact store, and renderer end-to-end.

Covers all extractor paths:
- deterministic skills (stammdaten, bank-csv)
- LLM-backed extractors (email, invoice, letter) using a scripted client that
  routes by payload_hash, so no API key is required.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import hashlib
import json
import sys
from collections.abc import Mapping
from email import message_from_string
from email.message import Message
from email.policy import default as default_policy
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from httpx import ASGITransport, AsyncClient

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.llm.client import LLMClient
from app.main import app
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
DATA_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "data"
STAMMDATEN_PATH = DATA_DIR / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"
BANK_CSV_PATH = DATA_DIR / "bank" / "kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv"
INC_DAY01 = DATA_DIR / "incremental" / "day-01"
EMAIL_DIR = INC_DAY01 / "emails" / "2026-01"
INVOICE_DIR = INC_DAY01 / "rechnungen" / "2026-01"
BRIEFE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "briefe" / "2025-12"

ORG_ID = "org_demo_berlin"
PROPERTY_ID = "LIE-001"
BANK_ROW_LIMIT = 200


def _payload_hash(blob: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(blob.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def _decode_email(path: Path) -> tuple[str, Message]:
    raw = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    msg = message_from_string(raw, policy=default_policy)
    return raw, msg


def _email_body(msg: Message) -> str:
    if msg.is_multipart():
        for part in msg.walk():
            if part.get_content_type() == "text/plain":
                return part.get_content()
    payload = msg.get_content() if msg.get_content_type() == "text/plain" else msg.get_payload()
    return payload if isinstance(payload, str) else ""


def _email_summary(path: Path) -> tuple[str, str, str, str]:
    raw, msg = _decode_email(path)
    subject = str(msg.get("Subject", "")).strip()
    sender = str(msg.get("From", "")).strip()
    body = _email_body(msg).strip()
    return raw, subject, sender, body


def _normalized_email_payload(path: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Build the normalized payload string we ingest, plus its hash."""
    _, subject, sender, body = _email_summary(path)
    text = f"From: {sender}\nSubject: {subject}\n\n{body}\n"
    return text, _payload_hash(text)


class ScriptedLLMClient:
    """Routes by substring (payload_hash) found in the user prompt."""

    def __init__(self, scripts: Mapping[str, str]) -> None:
        self._scripts = dict(scripts)

    async def complete(
        self,
        *,
        system_prompt: str,
        user_prompt: str,
        model: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        del system_prompt, model
        for key, response in self._scripts.items():
            if key in user_prompt:
                return response
        return json.dumps({"facts": [], "gap_signals": []})


def _email_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str]], dict[str, str]]:
    """Pick a few real emails, build canned LLM JSON keyed by payload_hash."""
    candidates = [
        ("20260101_083800_EMAIL-06547.eml", "EMAIL-06547"),
        ("20260101_110800_EMAIL-06548.eml", "EMAIL-06548"),
        ("20260102_103800_EMAIL-06550.eml", "EMAIL-06550"),
    ]
    canned: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
        (
            "EMAIL-06547",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "disputes",
                        "key": "REDACTED",
                        "value": {
                            "owner": "Oswald Gröttner",
                            "amount_eur": 4264,
                            "topic": "Sonderumlage",
                            "stance": "Einspruch",
                        },
                        "locator": "body",
                        "confidence": 0.78,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [
                    {
                        "section": "disputes",
                        "kind": "low_confidence",
                        "message": "Beschluss-Datum nicht angegeben",
                    }
                ],
            },
        ),
        (
            "EMAIL-06548",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "tenants",
                        "key": "REDACTED",
                        "value": {
                            "tenant": "Joanna Schäfer",
                            "unit": "WE-49",
                            "weeks_since_move_out": 7,
                            "request": "Auszahlung Kaution",
                        },
                        "locator": "body",
                        "confidence": 0.82,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [],
            },
        ),
        (
            "EMAIL-06550",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "communications",
                        "key": "reply_sonderumlage_groettner",
                        "value": {
                            "from": "Thomas Kreuzer",
                            "to": "Oswald Gröttner",
                            "subject": "Re: Sonderumlage - Einspruch",
                            "status": "Pruefung zugesagt",
                        },
                        "locator": "body",
                        "confidence": 0.7,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [],
            },
        ),
    ]

    chosen: list[tuple[Path, str]] = []
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {}
    canned_by_label = dict(canned)
    for filename, label in candidates:
        path = EMAIL_DIR / filename
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text, payload_hash = _normalized_email_payload(path)
        chosen.append((path, label))
        scripts[payload_hash] = json.dumps(canned_by_label[label])
        del text
    return chosen, scripts


def _invoice_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str, str]], dict[str, str]]:
    """Pick PDF invoices, build a synthetic text payload + canned response.

    The pipeline accepts text payloads for the `invoice` extractor; we use the
    filename-derived metadata as the body since PDFs are binary.
    """
    candidates: list[tuple[str, str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
        (
            "20260101_DL-001_INV-00195.pdf",
            "INV-00195",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "invoices",
                        "key": "inv_00195_2026-01",
                        "value": {
                            "invoice_id": "INV-00195",
                            "supplier": "DL-001",
                            "issue_date": "2026-01-01",
                            "currency": "EUR",
                        },
                        "locator": "filename",
                        "confidence": 0.6,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [
                    {
                        "section": "invoices",
                        "kind": "missing",
                        "message": "Betrag fehlt im PDF-Stub",
                    }
                ],
            },
        ),
    ]
    chosen: list[tuple[Path, str, str]] = []
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {}
    for filename, label, payload in candidates:
        path = INVOICE_DIR / filename
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text = (
            f"Invoice file: {filename}\n"
            f"Supplier: {filename.split('_')[1]}\n"
            f"Issue date: 2026-01-01\n"
            f"Currency: EUR\n"
        )
        h = _payload_hash(text)
        chosen.append((path, label, text))
        scripts[h] = json.dumps(payload)
    return chosen, scripts


def _letter_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str, str]], dict[str, str]]:
    candidates: list[tuple[str, str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
        (
            "20251208_mahnung_LTR-0135.pdf",
            "LTR-0135",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "communications",
                        "key": "REDACTED",
                        "value": {
                            "letter_id": "LTR-0135",
                            "type": "Mahnung",
                            "issue_date": "2025-12-08",
                        },
                        "locator": "filename",
                        "confidence": 0.65,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [],
            },
        ),
    ]
    chosen: list[tuple[Path, str, str]] = []
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {}
    for filename, label, payload in candidates:
        path = BRIEFE_DIR / filename
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text = f"Letter file: {filename}\nType: Mahnung\nIssue date: 2025-12-08\n"
        h = _payload_hash(text)
        chosen.append((path, label, text))
        scripts[h] = json.dumps(payload)
    return chosen, scripts


def _read_stammdaten() -> tuple[dict[str, Any], str]:
    raw = STAMMDATEN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    data = json.loads(raw)
    return data, _payload_hash(raw)


def _read_bank_rows() -> tuple[str, str]:
    text = BANK_CSV_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    lines = text.splitlines()
    head = lines[: 1 + BANK_ROW_LIMIT]
    trimmed = "\n".join(head) + "\n"
    return trimmed, _payload_hash(trimmed)


async def _post_envelope(
    client: AsyncClient,
    *,
    source: str,
    kind: str,
    property_id: str,
    content_type: str,
    payload_uri: str,
    payload_hash: str,
    payload: Any,
    received_at: str = "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    body = {
        "envelope": {
            "source": source,
            "kind": kind,
            "org": ORG_ID,
            "property": property_id,
            "content_type": content_type,
            "payload_uri": payload_uri,
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": received_at,
        },
        "payload": payload,
    }
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()


def _summary_line(label: str, response: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    return (
        f"{label}: envelope={response['envelope_id'][:18]} "
        f"facts={len(response['fact_ids'])} "
        f"gaps={len(response['gap_event_ids'])} "
        f"observed={len(response['observation_event_ids'])}"
    )


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    print(f"# output_dir: {settings.output_dir}")

    emails, email_scripts = _email_scripts()
    invoices, invoice_scripts = _invoice_scripts()
    letters, letter_scripts = _letter_scripts()
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {**email_scripts, **invoice_scripts, **letter_scripts}

    llm_client: LLMClient = ScriptedLLMClient(scripts)
    plugin = build_plugin(llm_client=llm_client)
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    stammdaten, stammdaten_hash = _read_stammdaten()
    bank_csv, bank_hash = _read_bank_rows()

    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://demo") as client:
        first = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="stammdaten",
            kind="stammdaten",
            property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
            content_type="application/json",
            payload_uri="data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            payload_hash=stammdaten_hash,
            payload=stammdaten,
        )
        print(_summary_line("stammdaten", first))

        second = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="bank",
            kind="bank-csv",
            property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
            content_type="text/csv",
            payload_uri=f"data/bank/kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv#first-{BANK_ROW_LIMIT}",
            payload_hash=bank_hash,
            payload=bank_csv,
        )
        print(_summary_line(f"bank-csv ({BANK_ROW_LIMIT} rows)", second))

        for path, label in emails:
            text, payload_hash = _normalized_email_payload(path)
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="email",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/incremental/day-01/emails/2026-01/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=payload_hash,
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"email {label}", response))

        for path, label, text in invoices:
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="invoice",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/incremental/day-01/rechnungen/2026-01/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=_payload_hash(text),
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"invoice {label}", response))

        for path, label, text in letters:
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="letter",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/briefe/2025-12/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=_payload_hash(text),
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"letter {label}", response))

    building_md = settings.output_dir / PROPERTY_ID / "building.md"
    print(f"building.md: {building_md} (exists={building_md.exists()})")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text, payload_hash = _normalized_email_payload(path)
        chosen.append((path, label))
        scripts[payload_hash] = json.dumps(canned_by_label[label])
        del text
    return chosen, scripts


def _invoice_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str, str]], dict[str, str]]:
    """Pick PDF invoices, build a synthetic text payload + canned response.

    The pipeline accepts text payloads for the `invoice` extractor; we use the
    filename-derived metadata as the body since PDFs are binary.
    """
    candidates: list[tuple[str, str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
        (
            "20260101_DL-001_INV-00195.pdf",
            "INV-00195",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "invoices",
                        "key": "inv_00195_2026-01",
                        "value": {
                            "invoice_id": "INV-00195",
                            "supplier": "DL-001",
                            "issue_date": "2026-01-01",
                            "currency": "EUR",
                        },
                        "locator": "filename",
                        "confidence": 0.6,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [
                    {
                        "section": "invoices",
                        "kind": "missing",
                        "message": "Betrag fehlt im PDF-Stub",
                    }
                ],
            },
        ),
    ]
    chosen: list[tuple[Path, str, str]] = []
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {}
    for filename, label, payload in candidates:
        path = INVOICE_DIR / filename
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text = (
            f"Invoice file: {filename}\n"
            f"Supplier: {filename.split('_')[1]}\n"
            f"Issue date: 2026-01-01\n"
            f"Currency: EUR\n"
        )
        h = _payload_hash(text)
        chosen.append((path, label, text))
        scripts[h] = json.dumps(payload)
    return chosen, scripts


def _letter_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str, str]], dict[str, str]]:
    candidates: list[tuple[str, str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
        (
            "20251208_mahnung_LTR-0135.pdf",
            "LTR-0135",
            {
                "facts": [
                    {
                        "section": "communications",
                        "key": "REDACTED",
                        "value": {
                            "letter_id": "LTR-0135",
                            "type": "Mahnung",
                            "issue_date": "2025-12-08",
                        },
                        "locator": "filename",
                        "confidence": 0.65,
                    }
                ],
                "gap_signals": [],
            },
        ),
    ]
    chosen: list[tuple[Path, str, str]] = []
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {}
    for filename, label, payload in candidates:
        path = BRIEFE_DIR / filename
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        text = f"Letter file: {filename}\nType: Mahnung\nIssue date: 2025-12-08\n"
        h = _payload_hash(text)
        chosen.append((path, label, text))
        scripts[h] = json.dumps(payload)
    return chosen, scripts


def _read_stammdaten() -> tuple[dict[str, Any], str]:
    raw = STAMMDATEN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    data = json.loads(raw)
    return data, _payload_hash(raw)


def _read_bank_rows() -> tuple[str, str]:
    text = BANK_CSV_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    lines = text.splitlines()
    head = lines[: 1 + BANK_ROW_LIMIT]
    trimmed = "\n".join(head) + "\n"
    return trimmed, _payload_hash(trimmed)


async def _post_envelope(
    client: AsyncClient,
    *,
    source: str,
    kind: str,
    property_id: str,
    content_type: str,
    payload_uri: str,
    payload_hash: str,
    payload: Any,
    received_at: str = "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    body = {
        "envelope": {
            "source": source,
            "kind": kind,
            "org": ORG_ID,
            "property": property_id,
            "content_type": content_type,
            "payload_uri": payload_uri,
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": received_at,
        },
        "payload": payload,
    }
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()


def _summary_line(label: str, response: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    return (
        f"{label}: envelope={response['envelope_id'][:18]} "
        f"facts={len(response['fact_ids'])} "
        f"gaps={len(response['gap_event_ids'])} "
        f"observed={len(response['observation_event_ids'])}"
    )


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    print(f"# output_dir: {settings.output_dir}")

    emails, email_scripts = _email_scripts()
    invoices, invoice_scripts = _invoice_scripts()
    letters, letter_scripts = _letter_scripts()
    scripts: dict[str, str] = {**email_scripts, **invoice_scripts, **letter_scripts}

    llm_client: LLMClient = ScriptedLLMClient(scripts)
    plugin = build_plugin(llm_client=llm_client)
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    stammdaten, stammdaten_hash = _read_stammdaten()
    bank_csv, bank_hash = _read_bank_rows()

    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://demo") as client:
        first = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="stammdaten",
            kind="stammdaten",
            property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
            content_type="application/json",
            payload_uri="data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            payload_hash=stammdaten_hash,
            payload=stammdaten,
        )
        print(_summary_line("stammdaten", first))

        second = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="bank",
            kind="bank-csv",
            property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
            content_type="text/csv",
            payload_uri=f"data/bank/kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv#first-{BANK_ROW_LIMIT}",
            payload_hash=bank_hash,
            payload=bank_csv,
        )
        print(_summary_line(f"bank-csv ({BANK_ROW_LIMIT} rows)", second))

        for path, label in emails:
            text, payload_hash = _normalized_email_payload(path)
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="email",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/incremental/day-01/emails/2026-01/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=payload_hash,
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"email {label}", response))

        for path, label, text in invoices:
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="invoice",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/incremental/day-01/rechnungen/2026-01/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=_payload_hash(text),
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"invoice {label}", response))

        for path, label, text in letters:
            response = await _post_envelope(
                client,
                source=label,
                kind="letter",
                property_id=PROPERTY_ID,
                content_type="text/plain",
                payload_uri=f"data/briefe/2025-12/{path.name}",
                payload_hash=_payload_hash(text),
                payload=text,
            )
            print(_summary_line(f"letter {label}", response))

    building_md = settings.output_dir / PROPERTY_ID / "building.md"
    print(f"building.md: {building_md} (exists={building_md.exists()})")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

"""Ingest the bundled property_de fixture through the engine pipeline.

Hits the running ASGI app via httpx ASGITransport so the demo exercises the
real ingest route, observers, fact store, and renderer end-to-end.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import hashlib
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from httpx import ASGITransport, AsyncClient

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.main import app

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
STAMMDATEN_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"
BANK_CSV_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "bank" / "kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv"
ORG_ID = "org_demo_berlin"
BANK_ROW_LIMIT = 25


def _payload_hash(blob: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(blob.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


async def _post_envelope(
    client: AsyncClient,
    *,
    source: str,
    kind: str,
    property_id: str,
    content_type: str,
    payload_uri: str,
    payload_hash: str,
    payload: Any,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    body = {
        "envelope": {
            "source": source,
            "kind": kind,
            "org": ORG_ID,
            "property": property_id,
            "content_type": content_type,
            "payload_uri": payload_uri,
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        },
        "payload": payload,
    }
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()


def _read_stammdaten() -> tuple[dict[str, Any], str, str]:
    raw = STAMMDATEN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    data = json.loads(raw)
    property_id = str(data["liegenschaft"]["id"])
    return data, property_id, _payload_hash(raw)


def _read_bank_rows() -> tuple[str, str]:
    text = BANK_CSV_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    lines = text.splitlines()
    head = lines[: 1 + BANK_ROW_LIMIT]
    trimmed = "\n".join(head) + "\n"
    return trimmed, _payload_hash(trimmed)


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    print(f"# output_dir: {settings.output_dir}")

    stammdaten, property_id, stammdaten_hash = _read_stammdaten()
    bank_csv, bank_hash = _read_bank_rows()

    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://demo") as client:
        first = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="stammdaten",
            kind="stammdaten",
            property_id=property_id,
            content_type="application/json",
            payload_uri="data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            payload_hash=stammdaten_hash,
            payload=stammdaten,
        )
        print(
            f"stammdaten: envelope={first['envelope_id']} "
            f"facts={len(first['fact_ids'])}"
        )

        second = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="bank",
            kind="bank-csv",
            property_id=property_id,
            content_type="text/csv",
            payload_uri="data/bank/kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv",
            payload_hash=bank_hash,
            payload=bank_csv,
        )
        print(
            f"bank-csv: envelope={second['envelope_id']} "
            f"facts={len(second['fact_ids'])}"
        )

    building_md = settings.output_dir / property_id / "building.md"
    print(f"building.md: {building_md} (exists={building_md.exists()})")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

1	"""Ingest the bundled property_de fixture through the engine pipeline.
2	
3	Hits the running ASGI app via httpx ASGITransport so the demo exercises the
4	real ingest route, observers, fact store, and renderer end-to-end.
5	
6	Covers all extractor paths:
7	- deterministic skills (stammdaten, bank-csv)
8	- LLM-backed extractors (email, invoice, letter) using a scripted client that
9	  routes by payload_hash, so no API key is required.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import asyncio
15	import hashlib
16	import json
17	import quopri
18	import sys
19	from collections.abc import Mapping
20	from email import message_from_string
21	from email.message import Message
22	from pathlib import Path
23	from typing import Any
24	
25	from httpx import ASGITransport, AsyncClient
26	
27	from app.core.config import get_settings
28	from app.llm.client import LLMClient
29	from app.main import app
30	from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
31	from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
32	from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin
33	
34	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
35	DATA_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "data"
36	STAMMDATEN_PATH = DATA_DIR / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"
37	BANK_CSV_PATH = DATA_DIR / "bank" / "kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv"
38	INC_DAY01 = DATA_DIR / "incremental" / "day-01"
39	EMAIL_DIR = INC_DAY01 / "emails" / "2026-01"
40	INVOICE_DIR = INC_DAY01 / "rechnungen" / "2026-01"
41	BRIEFE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "briefe" / "2025-12"
42	
43	ORG_ID = "org_demo_berlin"
44	PROPERTY_ID = "LIE-001"
45	BANK_ROW_LIMIT = 200
46	
47	
48	def _payload_hash(blob: str) -> str:
49	    return hashlib.sha256(blob.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
50	
51	
52	def _email_body(msg: Message) -> str:
53	    payload = msg.get_payload()
54	    if isinstance(payload, list):
55	        for part in payload:
56	            if isinstance(part, Message) and part.get_content_type() == "text/plain":
57	                inner = part.get_payload()
58	                if isinstance(inner, str):
59	                    payload = inner
60	                    break
61	        else:
62	            return ""
63	    if not isinstance(payload, str):
64	        return ""
65	    if msg.get("Content-Transfer-Encoding", "").lower() == "quoted-printable":
66	        return quopri.decodestring(payload).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
67	    return payload
68	
69	
70	def _normalized_email_payload(path: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
71	    """Build normalized payload string we ingest, plus its hash."""
72	    raw = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
73	    msg = message_from_string(raw)
74	    subject = str(msg.get("Subject", "")).strip()
75	    sender = str(msg.get("From", "")).strip()
76	    body = _email_body(msg).strip()
77	    text = f"From: {sender}\nSubject: {subject}\n\n{body}\n"
78	    return text, _payload_hash(text)
79	
80	
81	class ScriptedLLMClient:
82	    """Routes by substring (payload_hash) found in the user prompt."""
83	
84	    def __init__(self, scripts: Mapping[str, str]) -> None:
85	        self._scripts = dict(scripts)
86	
87	    async def complete(
88	        self,
89	        *,
90	        system_prompt: str,
91	        user_prompt: str,
92	        model: str | None = None,
93	    ) -> str:
94	        del system_prompt, model
95	        for key, response in self._scripts.items():
96	            if key in user_prompt:
97	                return response
98	        return json.dumps({"facts": [], "gap_signals": []})
99	
100	
101	def _email_scripts() -> tuple[list[tuple[Path, str]], dict[str, str]]:
102	    """Pick a few real emails, build canned LLM JSON keyed by payload_hash."""
103	    candidates = [
104	        ("20260101_083800_EMAIL-06547.eml", "EMAIL-06547"),
105	        ("20260101_110800_EMAIL-06548.eml", "EMAIL-06548"),
106	        ("20260102_103800_EMAIL-06550.eml", "EMAIL-06550"),
107	    ]
108	    canned: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]] = [
109	        (
110	            "EMAIL-06547",
111	            {

131	                    }
132	                ],
133	            },

140	                        "section": "tenants",

142	                        "value": {

content.text:
## Done. Summary

**Frontend rewrite (sync to backend):**
- `api.ts` rewritten — calls real backend: `listActive`, `listPending`, `listObligations`, `getBuildingMd`, `getCite`, `postIngest`. Sends `X-Org-ID` header (persisted in localStorage).
- `App.tsx` rewritten — single page facts viewer: top bar (org input + property dropdown + refresh + theme), left rail (section filter), main tabs (Building.md / Active / Pending / Obligations), right rail (demo ingest buttons that POST real envelopes).
- `TopBar.tsx` simplified to match.
- Deleted dead components: `Tree`, `Query`, `Palette`, `McpSettings`, `HumanNotesEditor`, `Breadcrumb`, `FrontmatterCard`, `Viewer`, `pages/Ingest.tsx`, `lib/markdown.ts`, `lib/rehypeStripComments.ts`. Sub-route routing dropped from `main.tsx`.
- `npm run build` clean. Vite proxy already routes `/api` → `:8000`.

**Demo ingest rewrite:**
- `scripts/ingest_fixture.py` now exercises every extractor path end-to-end:
  - Stammdaten full → 60 active facts (deterministic skill)
  - Bank CSV 200 rows → 200 active facts (deterministic skill, was 25 before)
  - 3 real emails from `data/incremental/day-01/` → LLM extractor via `ScriptedLLMClient` (matches by `payload_hash`)
  - 1 invoice + 1 letter from `data/incremental/`/`data/briefe/` → same LLM path
- No external API key needed. `build_plugin(llm_client=ScriptedLLMClient(...))` reinstalled before posting.
- Building.md renders 10 sections: Bank, Buildings, Communications, Contacts, Disputes, Finance, Invoices, Property, Tenants, Units.

**Verification:** 80/80 pytest pass · ruff + ty clean · backend smoke (curl /active, /pending) returns expected facts · `npm run build` + `tsc` clean.

commit d4ca6b283b8494ed5dfb4cbffe687832ae786767
Author: mgorabbani <mgorabbani@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue May 5 00:29:18 2026 +0200

    refactor: vertical-engine architecture (phases 1-3)
    
    Replace wiki_chunks pipeline with vertical-agnostic engine + bundled
    property_de vertical. Engine modules under app/{core,api,mcp,ingest,llm,
    extract,skills,storage,render,plugin.py} never import app.verticals.*.
    
    Phase 1: Engine contracts (Envelope, Fact, ExtractionResult, Extractor,
    Skill, VerticalPlugin), bitemporal facts.duckdb sink, ingest router with
    payload-hash idempotency, async LLM client protocol with StaticLLMClient
    fake.
    
    Phase 2: Patcher upsert/supersede with event emission, active/by-section/
    as-of queries, render building.md from facts via collect_render_records
    that re-stamps activated-pending facts.
    
    Phase 3: property_de vertical with deterministic stammdaten/bank-csv/
    camt053 skills, LLM extractors for email/invoice/letter/booking,
    KnownSectionsObserver + LegalMapObserver emitting vocab_gap/legal_gap,
    hardcoded org allowlist via install_active_plugin registry.
    
    Cleanup: deleted app/services/handlers, wiki_chunks storage, legacy
    ask/sim/webhook/wiki/events/lint/properties routes, MCP server/tools/
    resources (to be rebuilt in phase 4), 60+ legacy tests. 54 passing tests
    remain (engine + verticals).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.text:
Big choice before I touch backend. Old backend = 90 files, 2660-line diff in refactor commit. 4 phases of new work (vertical-engine, bitemporal facts, MCP rebuild, hermes, arbio) sit on top.

Three paths:

**A. Full revert.** `git checkout d4ca6b2~1 -- app/ tests/ scripts/`. Restores old backend wholesale. Frontend works immediately. Loses phases 1–6 (vertical-engine, facts.duckdb, MCP, hermes, arbio). Lowest risk, biggest regression.

**B. Compat layer.** Keep new architecture. Add `/wiki/*`, `/ask`, `/sim/*`, `/wiki/human-notes` over current fact store. Fake the 9-stage pipeline events. Build wiki tree from sections. Heavy — ~1000 lines new code, vector ask + git ops to implement or stub.

**C. Hybrid.** Restore old `app/api/v1/{wiki,ask,sim}.py` + their `app/services/*` deps from `d4ca6b2~1`. Keep new `ingest`/`properties`/`facts`/`mcp` routes alongside. Two ingest paths coexist (wiki-files via sim, facts-store via /ingest). Old frontend uses sim path. Medium revert, both worlds intact.

Recommend **C**. Restores frontend immediately. Keeps vertical-engine work. Two pipelines diverge but neither breaks. Tradeoff: AGENTS.md says engine never imports verticals — the restored sim/handlers may violate that, will need to live outside the guard or mark exempt.

Pick A, B, or C?

content.text:
Agree. Old system had:
- Filesystem wiki + tree navigation
- Rich n8n-style streaming pipeline UI (9 stages, tool calls, scrubber)
- Vector ask
- Human notes editor with git commits per save
- Per-property pages with frontmatter, wikilinks, log.md, sources/

New system replaced all that with a flat facts list + building.md. Engine purity won, UX lost.

**Recommend Path A: full revert.** `git checkout d4ca6b2~1 -- app/ tests/ scripts/ pyproject.toml uv.lock`. Frontend works immediately. Phases 1-6 stay in git history — recoverable later if needed.

Question before I run it:
- Confirm full revert (drops vertical-engine, facts.duckdb, MCP rebuild, hermes, arbio)?
- Keep phases 1-6 on a side branch for later (`git branch wip/vertical-engine` before revert)?
- Revert AGENTS.md too, or leave the new doc?

Confirm.

attachment.snippet:
1	# AGENTS.md — PropContext
2	
3	Cross-tool memory file for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and any other agent. Source of truth for design pattern and code style. **Keep current.** When patterns change, update this file in the same commit.
4	
5	> `CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to this file. Do not duplicate.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What this repo is
10	
11	Living building memory for property management (German WEG domain). Pipeline ingests emails / invoices / bank tx / `stammdaten.json`, compresses into one `building.md` per property. External AI agent fetches `building.md` over HTTP. Full PRD: `PRD_Overview`. Hackathon scope: 48h MVP.
12	
13	## Tech stack (2026-04 baseline)
14	
15	| Layer | Choice | Version |
16	|---|---|---|
17	| Runtime | Python | 3.13+ |
18	| Pkg/env | uv | 0.11+ |
19	| API | FastAPI + uvicorn | 0.136.1 |
20	| Validation | Pydantic v2 | 2.13+ |
21	| Settings | pydantic-settings | 2.14+ |
22	| Logging | structlog | 25+ |
23	| HTTP client | httpx (async) | 0.28+ |
24	| Async fs | anyio | 4.7+ |
25	| Lint+format | ruff | 0.15+ |
26	| Type check | ty (Astral) | 0.0.32+ |
27	| Test | pytest + pytest-asyncio | 9 / 1.3 |
28	| Container | Docker (multi-stage, uv) | 29+ |
29	
30	Do not add libraries without first comparing 2-3 alternatives in chat and getting human sign-off. The ecosystem moves fast; "the classic choice" is usually wrong.
31	
32	## Layout
33	
34	```
35	app/
36	  main.py              # create_app(), lifespan, mounts /mcp
37	  api/v1/
38	    router.py          # mounts feature routers
39	    health.py
40	    buildings.py
41	  core/
42	    config.py          # Settings, get_settings (lru_cache)
43	    logging.py         # configure_logging
44	  mcp/                 # FastMCP server exposed at /mcp (org-scoped, OAuth via WorkOS)
45	    server.py          # build_mcp(settings) → FastMCP
46	    auth.py            # AuthKitProvider wiring
47	    context.py         # current_org_id, assert_property_access (per-call)
48	    orgs.py            # hardcoded org → property allowlist
49	    tools.py / resources.py / prompts.py
50	  schemas/             # Pydantic response/request models, no business logic
51	  services/            # business logic, IO, integrations
52	tests/
53	  conftest.py          # AsyncClient fixture, dependency_overrides
54	  test_*.py
55	data/                  # raw source dataset (read-only)
56	schema/                # extraction prompts + WIKI_SCHEMA
57	output/                # building.md files (written by pipeline, served by API)
58	```
59	
60	Rule: `api/` knows about `services/`, never the reverse. `services/` knows about external IO + `core/`. `schemas/` is leaf — imports nothing project-internal except other schemas.
61	
62	## Code style (non-negotiable)
63	
64	1. **`from __future__ import annotations` at the top of every `.py`** (except `__init__.py` if empty).
65	2. **Modern types only.** `list[str]` not `List[str]`. `X | None` not `Optional[X]`. `dict[K, V]` not `Dict`. No `typing.Tuple/List/Dict/Optional/Union` imports.
66	3. **Async by default for routes and IO services.** Blocking IO inside async = bug. Use `anyio` (or `asyncio.to_thread` for CPU-light blocking calls). No `requests` — `httpx.AsyncClient` only.
67	4. **Lifespan, not `@app.on_event`.** `on_event` is deprecated.
68	5. **Annotated DI.** Routes take `param: Annotated[T, Depends(provider)]`. Never bare `Depends()` in default arg.
69	6. **Pydantic v2 idioms.** `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(...)` / `ConfigDict(...)`. No nested `class Config`. Use `Field(default=...)`, not mutable defaults.
70	7. **Settings are immutable + cached.** One `get_settings()` `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)` factory. Routes get `Settings` only via `Depends(get_settings)` — never import the singleton.
71	8. **Structured logs only.** `log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)`. `log.info("event", key=value)`. No f-strings in log messages, no `print`.
72	9. **Errors raise `HTTPException`** with explicit status. Don't return error dicts.
73	10. **One `create_app()` factory.** Module-level `app = create_app()` is the ASGI entrypoint. No global mutation after import.
74	11. **Path validation at the edge.** Building IDs / user input go through Pydantic constraints (`Path(pattern=...)`, `StringConstraints`) before touching the filesystem. Never `Path(user_input)` raw.
75	12. **No comments stating *what*** the code does. Comment only the non-obvious *why*. Docstrings on public service classes/functions only.
76	
77	## Patterns
78	
79	### Adding a route
80	
81	1. New module in `app/api/v1/<feature>.py` exposing `router = APIRouter()`.
82	2. Register in `app/api/v1/router.py` with `prefix=` and `tags=`.
83	3. Pydantic response model in `app/schemas/<feature>.py`.
84	4. Business logic in `app/services/<feature>.py` with a `get_<feature>_service()` Depends provider.
85	5. Test in `tests/test_<feature>.py` using the `client` fixture.
86	
87	### Adding a setting
88	
89	1. Field on `Settings` in `app/core/config.py` with type + default.
90	2. Document in `.env.example` (create when first needed).
91	3. Override in tests via the `settings` fixture / `dependency_overrides`.
92	
93	### Adding a dependency
94	
95	```bash
96	uv add <pkg>            # runtime
97	uv add --group dev <pkg> # dev/test
98	```
99	
100	Pin a sane lower bound (`>=X.Y`). Never pin exact (`==`) except for security CVEs.
101	
102	### Adding an MCP capability
103	
104	1. Tool → `app/mcp/tools.py`. Use `assert_property_access(property_id)` at the top to enforce org scope. Raise `ToolError` for user-facing failure.
105	2. Resource → `app/mcp/resources.py`. Same access gate. URIs use `property://<id>` / `building://<prop>/<bld>` shape.
106	3. Prompt → `app/mcp/prompts.py`. Pure functions, no IO.
107	4. Org → property mapping is hardcoded in `app/mcp/orgs.py` for the MVP. When this moves to a database, the access gate stays the same — only `properties_for_org` changes.
108	5. Auth provider lives in `app/mcp/auth.py`. WorkOS AuthKit is the only supported AS today; swapping providers means changing this file alone (FastMCP `RemoteAuthProvider` contract).
109	6. Never read the bearer token directly. Use `app.mcp.context.current_org_id()` / `assert_property_access()` so per-call auth state is sourced from FastMCP's request context.
110	
111	## Commands
112	
113	```bash
114	# Install / sync env
115	uv sync
116	
117	# Dev server (hot reload, structured console logs)
118	uv run fastapi dev app/main.py
119	
120	# Prod-ish (no reload)
121	uv run fastapi run app/main.py
122	
123	# Tests
124	uv run pytest
125	uv run pytest -k buildings -vv
126	
127	# Lint / format / fix
128	uv run ruff check .
129	uv run ruff format .
130	uv run ruff check --fix .
131	
132	# Types
133	uv run ty check
134	```
135	
136	CI gate (when added) must run, in order: `ruff format --check`, `ruff check`, `ty check`, `pytest`.
137	
138	### Docker
139	
140	```bash
141	# Prod image (multi-stage, runs as non-root, healthcheck on /api/v1/health)
142	docker build -t propcontext:latest .
143	docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 propcontext:latest
144	
145	# Compose — prod profile (default)
146	docker compose up --build
147	
148	# Compose — dev profile (hot reload, source-mounted)
149	docker compose --profile dev up --build api-dev
150	```
151	
152	Image conventions:
153	- Builder stage uses `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.13-bookworm-slim`, `uv sync --frozen --no-dev`.
154	- Runtime is `python:3.13-slim-bookworm`, copies only `/app/.venv` + `app/`. No uv at runtime.
155	- Runs as uid 1000 (`app` user). Never run as root.
156	- `output/` is a volume — `building.md` files persist across container restarts; never bake them into the image.
157	- Settings read env vars with `APP_` prefix: `APP_ENV`, `APP_LOG_LEVEL`, `APP_OUTPUT_DIR`, etc.
158	
159	## Test conventions
160	
161	- `pytest-asyncio` in `auto` mode — async tests don't need `@pytest.mark.asyncio`.
162	- HTTP tests use `httpx.AsyncClient` with `ASGITransport(app=app)`. No live network.
163	- Override deps via `app.dependency_overrides[get_settings] = lambda: settings` in fixture, `clear()` in teardown.
164	- One assertion concept per test. Use `tmp_path` for filesystem.

... [31 lines truncated] ...

1	# Implementation Plan — PropContext
2	
3	Hackathon target: 48h MVP. Tests folded into each phase (TDD-style).
4	
5	## Decisions captured
6	
7	| Fork | Choice |
8	|---|---|
9	| MVP scope | Full pipeline, all event types |
10	| Output unit | Property tree on disk under `wiki/<LIE-id>/`; API serves `index.md` |
11	| Bootstrap | Hybrid — stammdaten skeleton → batch replay 2024-2025 → live deltas |
12	| Event delivery | `POST /webhook/ingest` (HMAC-signed) + dev replayer CLI |
13	| LLM stack | Anthropic API: Haiku 4.5 (classify) + Sonnet 4.6 (extract) |
14	| Indexing | DuckDB with FTS (German stemmer) for stammdaten, wiki_chunks, bank, invoices |
15	| Hermes loops | JSONL substrate only; inner/outer loops stubbed |
16	| Demo target | Live ingest + SSE view + reconciliation report |
17	| PDF parsing | Docling |
18	| Git scope | `wiki/` is its own git repo, commit per event |
19	| LLM in tests | `LLMClient` Protocol; `FakeLLMClient` injected via `dependency_overrides` |
20	
21	`LLMClient` is a `Protocol`; production binds to `AnthropicClient`, tests bind to `FakeLLMClient` returning canned responses keyed by `(model, prompt_hash)`. All injection via `app.dependency_overrides`.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Phase 0 — Foundations (~2h)
26	
27	- Deps: `uv add anthropic duckdb docling pypdf python-multipart`. Dev: `gitpython` (or shell out to git).
28	- `.env.example` with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `APP_WIKI_DIR`, `APP_NORMALIZE_DIR`, `APP_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET`.
29	- Extend `app/core/config.py`: `wiki_dir`, `normalize_dir`, `anthropic_api_key`, `webhook_hmac_secret`, `haiku_model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"`, `sonnet_model="claude-sonnet-4-6"`.
30	- Rename route: `app/api/v1/buildings.py` → `properties.py`. Endpoint becomes `GET /properties/{LIE-id}` returning `wiki/<LIE-id>/index.md`. Keep `/buildings/{HAUS-id}` as a sub-route returning `02_buildings/<HAUS-id>/index.md`.
31	- Request-id middleware bound into structlog context.
32	
33	**Tests:**
34	
35	- `tests/test_config.py` — settings load env vars with `APP_` prefix; `get_settings()` is cached.
36	- `tests/test_health.py` — already exists; update for new shape.
37	- `tests/test_properties.py` — `GET /properties/{LIE-id}` 404 on missing, 200 on present, rejects `LIE-../etc/passwd`.
38	
39	## Phase 1 — Storage layer (~3.5h)
40	
41	- `app/storage/stammdaten.py` — load `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json` into `stammdaten.duckdb` tables (`liegenschaft`, `gebaeude`, `einheiten`, `eigentuemer`, `mieter`, `dienstleister`). Indexes on email, IBAN, id.
42	- `app/storage/wiki_chunks.py` — `(property_id, file, section, body, entity_refs[], updated_at)`. FTS via DuckDB `fts_main_*` extension with German stemmer.
43	- `app/storage/bank.py` + `invoices.py` — load `bank_index.csv` and invoice metadata.
44	- `app/storage/idempotency.py` — `(event_id PK, received_at, status)`.
45	- All DBs created at app startup if missing; loader CLIs idempotent.
46	
47	**Tests:**
48	
49	- `tests/storage/test_stammdaten.py` — load fixture stammdaten.json → assert row counts (3 gebäude, 52 einheiten, 35 eigentümer); lookup by email/IBAN/id returns expected entities.
50	- `tests/storage/test_wiki_chunks.py` — insert sections → FTS query returns ranked hits; entity_refs filter works.
51	- `tests/storage/test_idempotency.py` — same `event_id` twice → second is rejected.
52	
53	## Phase 2 — Wiki bootstrap (~3.5h)
54	
55	- `app/tools/bootstrap_wiki.py` CLI. Reads stammdaten, emits skeleton:
56	  - `wiki/LIE-001/index.md` (Buildings, Bank Accounts, Open Issues, Recent Events, Procedural Memory, Provenance)
57	  - `02_buildings/HAUS-{12,13,…}/index.md` + `units/EH-NNN.md`
58	  - `03_people/eigentuemer/EIG-NNN.md`, `mieter/MIE-NNN.md`
59	  - `04_dienstleister/DL-NNN.md`
60	  - `05_finances/{overview,reconciliation}.md`, `06_skills.md`, `07_timeline.md`, `_state.json`, `log.md`
61	- Each file: `name`/`description` frontmatter + required sections + `# Human Notes` boundary.
62	- `git init wiki/` + initial commit `bootstrap: skeleton from stammdaten`.
63	
64	**Tests:**
65	
66	- `tests/tools/test_bootstrap.py` — run against fixture stammdaten in `tmp_path`. Assert: every required file exists; every file has valid `name`/`description` frontmatter; every required section heading present; `# Human Notes` boundary at EOF; `_state.json` valid JSON; `git log` shows one commit.
67	
68	## Phase 3 — Patcher (~5h)
69	
70	Critical foundation — everything downstream depends on Patcher correctness.
71	
72	- `app/services/patcher/ops.py` — `upsert_bullet`, `delete_bullet`, `upsert_row`, `delete_row`, `prepend_row`, `prune_ring`, `upsert_footnote`, `gc_footnotes`, `update_state`. Pure regex/line ops.
73	- `app/services/patcher/atomic.py` — tempfile → fsync → rename helper.
74	- `app/services/patcher/validate.py` — parses `schema/VOCABULARY.md`, validates every keyed value; refuses any byte offset past `# Human Notes`.
75	- `app/services/patcher/apply.py` — `apply_patch_plan(plan)`: validate → apply ops → append JSONL to `_hermes_feedback.jsonl` → `git commit -m "ingest(<event_id>): <summary>"`.
76	
77	**Tests:**
78	
79	- `tests/patcher/test_ops_upsert_bullet.py` — insert new key; update existing key; preserve unrelated bullets; preserve human bullets (no `**ID:**` prefix).
80	- `tests/patcher/test_ops_upsert_row.py` — same matrix for tables.

# Implementation Plan — PropContext

Hackathon target: 48h MVP. Tests folded into each phase (TDD-style).

## Decisions captured

| Fork | Choice |
|---|---|
| MVP scope | Full pipeline, all event types |
| Output unit | Property tree on disk under `wiki/<LIE-id>/`; API serves `index.md` |
| Bootstrap | Hybrid — stammdaten skeleton → batch replay 2024-2025 → live deltas |
| Event delivery | `POST /webhook/ingest` (HMAC-signed) + dev replayer CLI |
| LLM stack | Anthropic API: Haiku 4.5 (classify) + Sonnet 4.6 (extract) |
| Indexing | DuckDB with FTS (German stemmer) for stammdaten, wiki_chunks, bank, invoices |
| Hermes loops | JSONL substrate only; inner/outer loops stubbed |
| Demo target | Live ingest + SSE view + reconciliation report |
| PDF parsing | Docling |
| Git scope | `wiki/` is its own git repo, commit per event |
| LLM in tests | `LLMClient` Protocol; `FakeLLMClient` injected via `dependency_overrides` |

`LLMClient` is a `Protocol`; production binds to `AnthropicClient`, tests bind to `FakeLLMClient` returning canned responses keyed by `(model, prompt_hash)`. All injection via `app.dependency_overrides`.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundations (~2h)

- Deps: `uv add anthropic duckdb docling pypdf python-multipart`. Dev: `gitpython` (or shell out to git).
- `.env.example` with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `APP_WIKI_DIR`, `APP_NORMALIZE_DIR`, `APP_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET`.
- Extend `app/core/config.py`: `wiki_dir`, `normalize_dir`, `anthropic_api_key`, `webhook_hmac_secret`, `haiku_model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"`, `sonnet_model="claude-sonnet-4-6"`.
- Rename route: `app/api/v1/buildings.py` → `properties.py`. Endpoint becomes `GET /properties/{LIE-id}` returning `wiki/<LIE-id>/index.md`. Keep `/buildings/{HAUS-id}` as a sub-route returning `02_buildings/<HAUS-id>/index.md`.
- Request-id middleware bound into structlog context.

**Tests:**

- `tests/test_config.py` — settings load env vars with `APP_` prefix; `get_settings()` is cached.
- `tests/test_health.py` — already exists; update for new shape.
- `tests/test_properties.py` — `GET /properties/{LIE-id}` 404 on missing, 200 on present, rejects `LIE-../etc/passwd`.

## Phase 1 — Storage layer (~3.5h)

- `app/storage/stammdaten.py` — load `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json` into `stammdaten.duckdb` tables (`liegenschaft`, `gebaeude`, `einheiten`, `eigentuemer`, `mieter`, `dienstleister`). Indexes on email, IBAN, id.
- `app/storage/wiki_chunks.py` — `(property_id, file, section, body, entity_refs[], updated_at)`. FTS via DuckDB `fts_main_*` extension with German stemmer.
- `app/storage/bank.py` + `invoices.py` — load `bank_index.csv` and invoice metadata.
- `app/storage/idempotency.py` — `(event_id PK, received_at, status)`.
- All DBs created at app startup if missing; loader CLIs idempotent.

**Tests:**

- `tests/storage/test_stammdaten.py` — load fixture stammdaten.json → assert row counts (3 gebäude, 52 einheiten, 35 eigentümer); lookup by email/IBAN/id returns expected entities.
- `tests/storage/test_wiki_chunks.py` — insert sections → FTS query returns ranked hits; entity_refs filter works.
- `tests/storage/test_idempotency.py` — same `event_id` twice → second is rejected.

## Phase 2 — Wiki bootstrap (~3.5h)

- `app/tools/bootstrap_wiki.py` CLI. Reads stammdaten, emits skeleton:
  - `wiki/LIE-001/index.md` (Buildings, Bank Accounts, Open Issues, Recent Events, Procedural Memory, Provenance)
  - `02_buildings/HAUS-{12,13,…}/index.md` + `units/EH-NNN.md`
  - `03_people/eigentuemer/EIG-NNN.md`, `mieter/MIE-NNN.md`
  - `04_dienstleister/DL-NNN.md`
  - `05_finances/{overview,reconciliation}.md`, `06_skills.md`, `07_timeline.md`, `_state.json`, `log.md`
- Each file: `name`/`description` frontmatter + required sections + `# Human Notes` boundary.
- `git init wiki/` + initial commit `bootstrap: skeleton from stammdaten`.

**Tests:**

- `tests/tools/test_bootstrap.py` — run against fixture stammdaten in `tmp_path`. Assert: every required file exists; every file has valid `name`/`description` frontmatter; every required section heading present; `# Human Notes` boundary at EOF; `_state.json` valid JSON; `git log` shows one commit.

## Phase 3 — Patcher (~5h)

Critical foundation — everything downstream depends on Patcher correctness.

- `app/services/patcher/ops.py` — `upsert_bullet`, `delete_bullet`, `upsert_row`, `delete_row`, `prepend_row`, `prune_ring`, `upsert_footnote`, `gc_footnotes`, `update_state`. Pure regex/line ops.
- `app/services/patcher/atomic.py` — tempfile → fsync → rename helper.
- `app/services/patcher/validate.py` — parses `schema/VOCABULARY.md`, validates every keyed value; refuses any byte offset past `# Human Notes`.
- `app/services/patcher/apply.py` — `apply_patch_plan(plan)`: validate → apply ops → append JSONL to `_hermes_feedback.jsonl` → `git commit -m "ingest(<event_id>): <summary>"`.

**Tests:**

- `tests/patcher/test_ops_upsert_bullet.py` — insert new key; update existing key; preserve unrelated bullets; preserve human bullets (no `**ID:**` prefix).
- `tests/patcher/test_ops_upsert_row.py` — same matrix for tables.

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack, asynccontextmanager
from http import HTTPStatus
from pathlib import Path

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException as StarletteHTTPException
from starlette.responses import FileResponse, Response
from starlette.types import Lifespan, Scope

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp import build_mcp

PRM_LOCAL_PATH = "/mcp/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


class SPAStaticFiles(StaticFiles):
    """Static mount that falls back to index.html for unknown HTML paths (SPA routes)."""

    async def get_response(self, path: str, scope: Scope) -> Response:  # type: ignore[override]
        try:
            return await super().get_response(path, scope)
        except StarletteHTTPException as exc:
            if exc.status_code != HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND or "." in path.rsplit("/", 1)[-1]:
                raise
            directory = Path(str(self.directory)) if self.directory else None
            if directory is None:
                raise
            index = directory / "index.html"
            if not index.is_file():
                raise
            return FileResponse(index)


def _make_lifespan(child: Lifespan | None) -> Lifespan:
    @asynccontextmanager
    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
        settings = get_settings()
        configure_logging(settings)
        log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            if child is not None:
                await stack.enter_async_context(child(app))
            yield
        log.info("shutdown")

    return lifespan


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()

    mcp_app = None
    if settings.mcp_enabled:
        mcp_app = build_mcp(settings).http_app(path="/")

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_make_lifespan(mcp_app.lifespan if mcp_app else None),
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    if mcp_app is not None:

        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", include_in_schema=False)
        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp", include_in_schema=False)
        async def _prm_redirect() -> RedirectResponse:
            return RedirectResponse(url=PRM_LOCAL_PATH, status_code=307)

        app.mount("/mcp", mcp_app)
    static_dir = REPO_ROOT / "app" / "static"
    if static_dir.is_dir():
        app.mount("/", SPAStaticFiles(directory=static_dir, html=True), name="static")
    return app


app = create_app()

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack, asynccontextmanager
from http import HTTPStatus
from pathlib import Path

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException as StarletteHTTPException
from starlette.responses import FileResponse, Response
from starlette.types import Lifespan, Scope

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp import build_mcp

PRM_LOCAL_PATH = "/mcp/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


class SPAStaticFiles(StaticFiles):
    """Static mount that falls back to index.html for unknown HTML paths (SPA routes)."""

    async def get_response(self, path: str, scope: Scope) -> Response:  # type: ignore[override]
        try:
            return await super().get_response(path, scope)
        except StarletteHTTPException as exc:
            if exc.status_code != HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND or "." in path.rsplit("/", 1)[-1]:
                raise
            directory = Path(str(self.directory)) if self.directory else None
            if directory is None:
                raise
            index = directory / "index.html"
            if not index.is_file():
                raise
            return FileResponse(index)


def _make_lifespan(child: Lifespan | None) -> Lifespan:
    @asynccontextmanager
    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
        settings = get_settings()
        configure_logging(settings)
        log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            if child is not None:
                await stack.enter_async_context(child(app))
            yield
        log.info("shutdown")

    return lifespan


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()

    mcp_app = None
    if settings.mcp_enabled:
        mcp_app = build_mcp(settings).http_app(path="/")

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_make_lifespan(mcp_app.lifespan if mcp_app else None),
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    if mcp_app is not None:

        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", include_in_schema=False)
        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp", include_in_schema=False)
        async def _prm_redirect() -> RedirectResponse:
            return RedirectResponse(url=PRM_LOCAL_PATH, status_code=307)

        app.mount("/mcp", mcp_app)
    static_dir = REPO_ROOT / "app" / "static"
    if static_dir.is_dir():
        app.mount("/", SPAStaticFiles(directory=static_dir, html=True), name="static")
    return app


app = create_app()

1	# Ingestion Simulation
2	
3	This document simulates what happens after a new source event arrives and how
4	the markdown context is updated from start to finish.
5	
6	The product language says "one building.md per property." In the current code
7	and schema, that file is represented as `wiki/<property_id>/index.md`, with
8	supporting markdown files under the same property folder. Building-specific
9	markdown is served from `wiki/<property_id>/02_buildings/<building_id>/index.md`.
10	
11	## Current implementation snapshot
12	
13	The implemented ingestion path is:
14	
15	1. `POST /api/v1/webhook/ingest`
16	2. HMAC verification and `IngestEvent` validation
17	3. API-level idempotency claim in `output/idempotency.duckdb`
18	4. `Supervisor.handle(event)`
19	5. event handler normalization into `normalize/.../*.md`
20	6. signal classification through `classify_document`
21	7. entity resolution through `stammdaten.duckdb`
22	8. target-section lookup through `wiki_chunks.duckdb`
23	9. LLM extraction of a `PatchPlan`
24	10. conflict scan
25	11. deterministic patch application to `wiki/<property_id>/*.md`
26	12. `_hermes_feedback.jsonl` append
27	13. git commit in `wiki_dir`
28	14. reindex touched markdown files into `wiki_chunks.duckdb`
29	15. idempotency marked `done`
30	16. webhook response returned
31	
32	The schema docs also describe optional SSE broadcast and Hermes inner/outer
33	loops. Those are part of the intended concept, but they are not wired into the
34	current synchronous webhook code path yet.
35	
36	## Component map
37	
38	| Component | Code/docs | Role |
39	|---|---|---|
40	| Webhook API | `app/api/v1/webhook.py` | Accepts signed ingest events, rejects bad signatures, enforces replay protection, calls the supervisor. |
41	| Event schema | `app/schemas/webhook.py` | Defines `event_id`, `event_type`, `property_id`, optional `source_path`, and free-form `payload`. |
42	| Idempotency store | `app/storage/idempotency.py` | Stores `event_id` as `pending`, `done`, or `failed`; prevents duplicate webhook work. |
43	| Supervisor | `app/services/supervisor.py` | Orchestrates normalize, classify, resolve, locate, extract, conflict scan, patch, reindex. |
44	| Event handlers | `app/services/handlers/*` | Convert source-specific inputs into normalized markdown. Current direct handler keys include `email`, `eml`, `invoice`, `bank`, `letter`, `manual`, `document`, `schedule`, `lint`, `erp`, `chat`, `slack`, `whatsapp`, and `voicenote`. |
45	| Normalizers | `app/services/normalize/*` | Parse `.eml`, invoice/letter PDFs, and bank rows into markdown under `normalize/`. |
46	| Classifier | `app/services/classify.py` | Uses a small LLM prompt over sender, subject, and excerpt to decide signal/noise. No signal means no wiki patch. |
47	| Resolver | `app/services/resolve.py` | Uses email, IBAN, and explicit IDs to map a source to `MIE-*`, `EIG-*`, `EH-*`, `HAUS-*`, `DL-*`, and source IDs. |
48	| Stammdaten store | `app/storage/stammdaten.py` | DuckDB cache loaded from `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json`; source of entity identity. |
49	| Wiki chunks | `app/storage/wiki_chunks.py`, `app/services/reindex.py` | Derived DuckDB index of markdown sections. Used to find likely target sections and for search. |
50	| Extractor | `app/services/extract.py` | Sends normalized source plus located sections to the larger LLM and receives a canonical `PatchPlan`. |
51	| Conflict scanner | `app/services/conflict.py` | Defers risky overwrites such as status flips, large date drift, or amount deltas to `_pending_review.md`. |
52	| Patcher | `app/services/patcher/apply.py`, `app/services/patcher/ops.py` | Applies line-based markdown operations. No LLM at apply time. Refuses writes outside managed sections before `# Human Notes`. |
53	| Atomic writer | `app/services/patcher/atomic.py` | Writes via temp file, fsync, and replace. |
54	| Vocabulary | `schema/VOCABULARY.md` | Controlled values checked before patching. Unknown values become pending-review items. |
55	| Wiki schema | `schema/WIKI_SCHEMA.md`, `schema/CLAUDE.md` | Canonical markdown layout, sections, patch operation rules, conflict policy, and long-term Hermes plan. |
56	| Read API | `app/api/v1/properties.py`, `app/services/wiki.py` | Serves property and building markdown as `text/markdown`. |
57	
58	Note: schema examples use dotted event names like `email.received`. The current
59	handler registry does not map dotted names yet; `email.received` would fall
60	back to `DocumentHandler` unless the registry is expanded. This simulation uses
61	the current code-compatible event type `email`.
62	
63	## Scenario
64	
65	A tenant reports a heating outage in unit `EH-014`. The source adapter sends a
66	signed webhook event for property `LIE-001`.
67	
68	Sample webhook body:
69	
70	```json
71	{
72	  "event_id": "EMAIL-12044",
73	  "event_type": "email",
74	  "property_id": "LIE-001",
75	  "source_path": "data/emails/2026-04/20260425_143200_EMAIL-12044.eml",
76	  "payload": {}
77	}
78	```
79	
80	Assumed raw email content:
81	
82	```text
83	From: anna.mueller@example.test
84	To: pm@example.test
85	Date: Sat, 25 Apr 2026 14:32:00 +0200
86	Subject: Heizung kalt in EH-014
87	
88	Hallo,
89	in EH-014 ist die Heizung seit heute Mittag kalt. Bitte schicken Sie den
90	Heizungsdienst. Die Wohnung ist in HAUS-12.
91	Viele Gruesse
92	Anna Mueller
93	```
94	
95	## Timestamped event log
96	
97	| Time | Step | Component | What happened | Data read/written |
98	|---|---|---|---|---|
99	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.000+02:00 | source event | Mail adapter | New tenant email is detected and mapped to `event_id=EMAIL-12044`. | Reads mailbox; prepares webhook JSON. |
100	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.180+02:00 | webhook received | FastAPI webhook | `POST /api/v1/webhook/ingest` receives raw bytes. | Reads request body. |
101	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.184+02:00 | verify signature | `_verify_hmac` | Computes HMAC SHA-256 using `APP_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET`; compares with `x-propcontext-signature`. | No files written. |
102	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.188+02:00 | parse event | `_parse_event` | JSON is parsed into `IngestEvent`. | In-memory event object. |
103	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.194+02:00 | claim event | `IdempotencyStore.claim` | `EMAIL-12044` is inserted as `pending`. | Writes `output/idempotency.duckdb`. |
104	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.205+02:00 | dispatch | `get_event_handler` | `event_type=email` routes to `EmailHandler`. | In-memory handler selection. |
105	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.215+02:00 | normalize | `EmailHandler` and `normalize_eml` | Source `.eml` is parsed with Python email parser. | Reads `data/emails/2026-04/20260425_143200_EMAIL-12044.eml`. |
106	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.265+02:00 | write normalized source | `write_normalized_markdown` | Normalized markdown is written with source, sha256, parser, parsed time, mime, language. | Writes `normalize/eml/2026-04/EMAIL-12044.md`. |
107	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.310+02:00 | classify prompt | `classify_document` | Classifier sees sender, subject, and first 500 chars. | Sends LLM request using `settings.haiku_model`. |
108	| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.980+02:00 | classify result | `Classification` | Email is considered signal: category `task_update/heating`, priority `high`, confidence `0.93`. | No files written. |
109	| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.005+02:00 | open master data | `_open_stammdaten` | `stammdaten.duckdb` is opened; if empty, JSON master data is loaded. | Reads `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json`; writes `output/stammdaten.duckdb` if needed. |
110	| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.035+02:00 | resolve entities | `resolve_context` | Sender email maps to tenant, explicit `EH-014` and `HAUS-12` are validated. Related owner/unit/building chain is collected. | In-memory `ResolutionResult`. |
111	| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.065+02:00 | open section index | `open_wiki_chunks` | The wiki section cache is opened. If the property wiki exists but has no rows, it is reindexed. | Reads/writes `output/wiki_chunks.duckdb`. |
112	| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.090+02:00 | locate sections | `locate_sections` | Finds candidate sections by entity refs first, then full-text query fallback. | Reads `wiki_chunks` rows. |
113	| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.130+02:00 | extraction prompt | `extract_patch_plan` | Sends normalized email, resolved IDs, source IDs, and located section bodies to extractor. | Sends LLM request using `settings.sonnet_model`. |
114	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.420+02:00 | patch plan returned | `canonicalize_patch_plan` | LLM output is normalized: wiki-prefixed paths become property-relative paths, `content` becomes `text` or `row`. | In-memory `PatchPlan`. |
115	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.455+02:00 | conflict scan | `scan_patch_plan_conflicts` | Existing keyed lines are checked for status flip, date drift, or amount delta. No conflict found. | Reads target markdown files. |
116	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.480+02:00 | vocabulary validation | `validate_keyed_values` | Controlled values are checked. Invalid ops would be moved to `_pending_review.md`. | Reads `schema/VOCABULARY.md`. |
117	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.505+02:00 | patch open issue | `upsert_bullet` | Inserts or replaces the keyed `EH-014` issue line in `HAUS-12/index.md`. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/index.md`. |
118	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.535+02:00 | patch recent event | `prepend_row` | Adds newest event row at top of the `Recent Events` table. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/index.md`. |
119	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.565+02:00 | patch unit history | `prepend_row` | Adds the event to the unit dossier history. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/units/EH-014.md`. |
120	| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.595+02:00 | patch tenant contact | `prepend_row` | Adds the email to tenant contact history. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/03_people/mieter/MIE-014.md`. |

file.content:
# Ingestion Simulation

This document simulates what happens after a new source event arrives and how
the markdown context is updated from start to finish.

The product language says "one building.md per property." In the current code
and schema, that file is represented as `wiki/<property_id>/index.md`, with
supporting markdown files under the same property folder. Building-specific
markdown is served from `wiki/<property_id>/02_buildings/<building_id>/index.md`.

## Current implementation snapshot

The implemented ingestion path is:

1. `POST /api/v1/webhook/ingest`
2. HMAC verification and `IngestEvent` validation
3. API-level idempotency claim in `output/idempotency.duckdb`
4. `Supervisor.handle(event)`
5. event handler normalization into `normalize/.../*.md`
6. signal classification through `classify_document`
7. entity resolution through `stammdaten.duckdb`
8. target-section lookup through `wiki_chunks.duckdb`
9. LLM extraction of a `PatchPlan`
10. conflict scan
11. deterministic patch application to `wiki/<property_id>/*.md`
12. `_hermes_feedback.jsonl` append
13. git commit in `wiki_dir`
14. reindex touched markdown files into `wiki_chunks.duckdb`
15. idempotency marked `done`
16. webhook response returned

The schema docs also describe optional SSE broadcast and Hermes inner/outer
loops. Those are part of the intended concept, but they are not wired into the
current synchronous webhook code path yet.

## Component map

| Component | Code/docs | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Webhook API | `app/api/v1/webhook.py` | Accepts signed ingest events, rejects bad signatures, enforces replay protection, calls the supervisor. |
| Event schema | `app/schemas/webhook.py` | Defines `event_id`, `event_type`, `property_id`, optional `source_path`, and free-form `payload`. |
| Idempotency store | `app/storage/idempotency.py` | Stores `event_id` as `pending`, `done`, or `failed`; prevents duplicate webhook work. |
| Supervisor | `app/services/supervisor.py` | Orchestrates normalize, classify, resolve, locate, extract, conflict scan, patch, reindex. |
| Event handlers | `app/services/handlers/*` | Convert source-specific inputs into normalized markdown. Current direct handler keys include `email`, `eml`, `invoice`, `bank`, `letter`, `manual`, `document`, `schedule`, `lint`, `erp`, `chat`, `slack`, `whatsapp`, and `voicenote`. |
| Normalizers | `app/services/normalize/*` | Parse `.eml`, invoice/letter PDFs, and bank rows into markdown under `normalize/`. |
| Classifier | `app/services/classify.py` | Uses a small LLM prompt over sender, subject, and excerpt to decide signal/noise. No signal means no wiki patch. |
| Resolver | `app/services/resolve.py` | Uses email, IBAN, and explicit IDs to map a source to `MIE-*`, `EIG-*`, `EH-*`, `HAUS-*`, `DL-*`, and source IDs. |
| Stammdaten store | `app/storage/stammdaten.py` | DuckDB cache loaded from `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json`; source of entity identity. |
| Wiki chunks | `app/storage/wiki_chunks.py`, `app/services/reindex.py` | Derived DuckDB index of markdown sections. Used to find likely target sections and for search. |
| Extractor | `app/services/extract.py` | Sends normalized source plus located sections to the larger LLM and receives a canonical `PatchPlan`. |
| Conflict scanner | `app/services/conflict.py` | Defers risky overwrites such as status flips, large date drift, or amount deltas to `_pending_review.md`. |
| Patcher | `app/services/patcher/apply.py`, `app/services/patcher/ops.py` | Applies line-based markdown operations. No LLM at apply time. Refuses writes outside managed sections before `# Human Notes`. |
| Atomic writer | `app/services/patcher/atomic.py` | Writes via temp file, fsync, and replace. |
| Vocabulary | `schema/VOCABULARY.md` | Controlled values checked before patching. Unknown values become pending-review items. |
| Wiki schema | `schema/WIKI_SCHEMA.md`, `schema/CLAUDE.md` | Canonical markdown layout, sections, patch operation rules, conflict policy, and long-term Hermes plan. |
| Read API | `app/api/v1/properties.py`, `app/services/wiki.py` | Serves property and building markdown as `text/markdown`. |

Note: schema examples use dotted event names like `email.received`. The current
handler registry does not map dotted names yet; `email.received` would fall
back to `DocumentHandler` unless the registry is expanded. This simulation uses
the current code-compatible event type `email`.

## Scenario

A tenant reports a heating outage in unit `EH-014`. The source adapter sends a
signed webhook event for property `LIE-001`.

Sample webhook body:

```json
{
  "event_id": "EMAIL-12044",
  "event_type": "email",
  "property_id": "LIE-001",
  "source_path": "data/emails/2026-04/20260425_143200_EMAIL-12044.eml",
  "payload": {}
}
```

Assumed raw email content:

```text
From: anna.mueller@example.test
To: pm@example.test
Date: Sat, 25 Apr 2026 14:32:00 +0200
Subject: Heizung kalt in EH-014

Hallo,
in EH-014 ist die Heizung seit heute Mittag kalt. Bitte schicken Sie den
Heizungsdienst. Die Wohnung ist in HAUS-12.
Viele Gruesse
Anna Mueller
```

## Timestamped event log

| Time | Step | Component | What happened | Data read/written |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.000+02:00 | source event | Mail adapter | New tenant email is detected and mapped to `event_id=EMAIL-12044`. | Reads mailbox; prepares webhook JSON. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.180+02:00 | webhook received | FastAPI webhook | `POST /api/v1/webhook/ingest` receives raw bytes. | Reads request body. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.184+02:00 | verify signature | `_verify_hmac` | Computes HMAC SHA-256 using `APP_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET`; compares with `x-propcontext-signature`. | No files written. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.188+02:00 | parse event | `_parse_event` | JSON is parsed into `IngestEvent`. | In-memory event object. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.194+02:00 | claim event | `IdempotencyStore.claim` | `EMAIL-12044` is inserted as `pending`. | Writes `output/idempotency.duckdb`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.205+02:00 | dispatch | `get_event_handler` | `event_type=email` routes to `EmailHandler`. | In-memory handler selection. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.215+02:00 | normalize | `EmailHandler` and `normalize_eml` | Source `.eml` is parsed with Python email parser. | Reads `data/emails/2026-04/20260425_143200_EMAIL-12044.eml`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.265+02:00 | write normalized source | `write_normalized_markdown` | Normalized markdown is written with source, sha256, parser, parsed time, mime, language. | Writes `normalize/eml/2026-04/EMAIL-12044.md`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.310+02:00 | classify prompt | `classify_document` | Classifier sees sender, subject, and first 500 chars. | Sends LLM request using `settings.haiku_model`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:00.980+02:00 | classify result | `Classification` | Email is considered signal: category `task_update/heating`, priority `high`, confidence `0.93`. | No files written. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.005+02:00 | open master data | `_open_stammdaten` | `stammdaten.duckdb` is opened; if empty, JSON master data is loaded. | Reads `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json`; writes `output/stammdaten.duckdb` if needed. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.035+02:00 | resolve entities | `resolve_context` | Sender email maps to tenant, explicit `EH-014` and `HAUS-12` are validated. Related owner/unit/building chain is collected. | In-memory `ResolutionResult`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.065+02:00 | open section index | `open_wiki_chunks` | The wiki section cache is opened. If the property wiki exists but has no rows, it is reindexed. | Reads/writes `output/wiki_chunks.duckdb`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.090+02:00 | locate sections | `locate_sections` | Finds candidate sections by entity refs first, then full-text query fallback. | Reads `wiki_chunks` rows. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:01.130+02:00 | extraction prompt | `extract_patch_plan` | Sends normalized email, resolved IDs, source IDs, and located section bodies to extractor. | Sends LLM request using `settings.sonnet_model`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.420+02:00 | patch plan returned | `canonicalize_patch_plan` | LLM output is normalized: wiki-prefixed paths become property-relative paths, `content` becomes `text` or `row`. | In-memory `PatchPlan`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.455+02:00 | conflict scan | `scan_patch_plan_conflicts` | Existing keyed lines are checked for status flip, date drift, or amount delta. No conflict found. | Reads target markdown files. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.480+02:00 | vocabulary validation | `validate_keyed_values` | Controlled values are checked. Invalid ops would be moved to `_pending_review.md`. | Reads `schema/VOCABULARY.md`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.505+02:00 | patch open issue | `upsert_bullet` | Inserts or replaces the keyed `EH-014` issue line in `HAUS-12/index.md`. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/index.md`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.535+02:00 | patch recent event | `prepend_row` | Adds newest event row at top of the `Recent Events` table. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/index.md`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.565+02:00 | patch unit history | `prepend_row` | Adds the event to the unit dossier history. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/02_buildings/HAUS-12/units/EH-014.md`. |
| 2026-04-25T14:32:03.595+02:00 | patch tenant contact | `prepend_row` | Adds the email to tenant contact history. | Writes `wiki/LIE-001/03_people/mieter/MIE-014.md`. |

107	- `app/services/classify.py` — Haiku call on sender + subject + first 500 chars → `{signal, category, priority, confidence}`. ~90% short-circuit at `signal=false`.
108	- `app/services/resolve.py` — deterministic stammdaten lookups; sender email → `MIE-/EIG-/DL-id`, IBAN → `DL/EIG`, mentioned `EH-/INV-/LTR-` ids validated.

Each is a thin shim picking the right normalizer + telling Supervisor which event-type-specific extras to pass.
